categories.name: Sole Proprietorship
categories.description: A business owned and operated by a single individual with no legal distinction between owner and business.

categories.name: Partnership
categories.description: A business arrangement where two or more individuals share ownership and management responsibilities.

categories.name: Limited Liability Entity
categories.description: A business structure that provides personal liability protection for its owners.

categories.name: Corporation
categories.description: A legal entity that is separate and distinct from its owners, offering the strongest personal liability protection.

categories.name: Non-Profit Organization
categories.description: An organization that operates for purposes other than generating profit for owners or shareholders.

categories.name: Cooperative
categories.description: A business owned and operated by its members who share the benefits and responsibilities.

categories.name: Trust
categories.description: A fiduciary arrangement in which assets are managed by a trustee for the benefit of beneficiaries.

categories.name: Government Entity
categories.description: A business or organization owned or controlled by a government body.

SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP.name: Individual Entrepreneur
SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP.description: A self-employed individual running a business independently.

SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP.name: Freelancer
SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP.description: An independent contractor offering services to clients without long-term commitment.

SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP.name: Self-Employed
SOLE_PROPRIETORSHIP.description: An individual who works for themselves rather than an employer.

PARTNERSHIP.name: General Partnership
PARTNERSHIP.description: A partnership where all partners share equal management rights and liability.

PARTNERSHIP.name: Limited Partnership
PARTNERSHIP.description: A partnership with at least one general partner and one or more limited partners.

PARTNERSHIP.name: Limited Liability Partnership
PARTNERSHIP.description: A partnership that limits partners' personal liability for the business's debts.

LIMITED_LIABILITY.name: Single-Member LLC
LIMITED_LIABILITY.description: A limited liability company owned by a single individual.

LIMITED_LIABILITY.name: Multi-Member LLC
LIMITED_LIABILITY.description: A limited liability company owned by two or more individuals.

LIMITED_LIABILITY.name: Professional LLC
LIMITED_LIABILITY.description: A limited liability company formed by licensed professionals.

CORPORATION.name: C Corporation
CORPORATION.description: A standard corporation taxed separately from its owners.

CORPORATION.name: S Corporation
CORPORATION.description: A corporation with pass-through taxation, avoiding double taxation.

CORPORATION.name: Public Limited Company
CORPORATION.description: A corporation whose shares can be traded publicly on a stock exchange.

CORPORATION.name: Private Limited Company
CORPORATION.description: A corporation whose shares are not publicly traded.

CORPORATION.name: One Person Company
CORPORATION.description: A corporation with a single person as both the director and shareholder.

NON_PROFIT.name: Charitable Organization
NON_PROFIT.description: An organization focused on charitable purposes and public benefit.

NON_PROFIT.name: Foundation
NON_PROFIT.description: A non-profit that typically makes grants to other organizations or individuals.

NON_PROFIT.name: Association
NON_PROFIT.description: An organization formed by individuals with a common interest or purpose.

NON_PROFIT.name: Non-Governmental Organization
NON_PROFIT.description: A non-profit organization operating independently of any government.

COOPERATIVE.name: Worker Cooperative
COOPERATIVE.description: A cooperative owned and managed by its worker-members.

COOPERATIVE.name: Consumer Cooperative
COOPERATIVE.description: A cooperative owned by its customers who share the benefits.

COOPERATIVE.name: Agricultural Cooperative
COOPERATIVE.description: A cooperative formed by farmers to improve their marketing power.

TRUST.name: Revocable Trust
TRUST.description: A trust that can be modified or revoked by the grantor during their lifetime.

TRUST.name: Irrevocable Trust
TRUST.description: A trust that cannot be modified or terminated without the beneficiary's permission.

TRUST.name: Business Trust
TRUST.description: A trust established for commercial purposes.

GOVERNMENT.name: Federal Agency
GOVERNMENT.description: An organization that is a part of the federal government.

GOVERNMENT.name: State Agency
GOVERNMENT.description: An organization that is a part of a state or provincial government.

GOVERNMENT.name: Municipal Entity
GOVERNMENT.description: An organization that is a part of local government.

IN.name: Private Limited Company
IN.description: A privately held company with limited liability for shareholders.

IN.name: Public Limited Company
IN.description: A company whose shares can be publicly traded.

IN.name: One Person Company
IN.description: A company with a single person as owner and director.

IN.name: Limited Liability Partnership
IN.description: A partnership that limits partners' personal liability.

IN.name: Partnership Firm
IN.description: A business formed by two or more partners.

IN.name: Sole Proprietorship
IN.description: A business owned and operated by a single person.

IN.name: Trust
IN.description: A fiduciary arrangement for charitable or business purposes.

IN.name: Society
IN.description: An organization registered under the Societies Registration Act.

IN.name: Section 8 Company
IN.description: A company established for charitable purposes under Section 8 of the Companies Act.

IN.name: Nidhi Company
IN.description: A type of NBFC that deals with deposits and lending among members.

IN.name: Producer Company
IN.description: A company formed by agricultural producers.

US.name: Limited Liability Company
US.description: A flexible business structure providing personal liability protection.

US.name: C Corporation
US.description: A standard corporation taxed separately from its owners.

US.name: S Corporation
US.description: A corporation with pass-through tax treatment.

US.name: Sole Proprietorship
US.description: The simplest business structure for individual owners.

US.name: General Partnership
US.description: A partnership where all partners share equal responsibility.

US.name: Limited Partnership
US.description: A partnership with limited and general partners.

US.name: Limited Liability Partnership
US.description: A partnership that protects partners from personal liability.

US.name: Non-Profit Corporation
US.description: A corporation organized for non-profit purposes.

US.name: Cooperative
US.description: A business owned and operated by its members.

US.name: Professional Corporation
US.description: A corporation formed by licensed professionals.

US.name: Benefit Corporation
US.description: A for-profit corporation committed to public benefit.

GB.name: Private Limited Company
GB.description: A company whose shares are privately held.

GB.name: Public Limited Company
GB.description: A company that can offer shares to the general public.

GB.name: Limited Liability Partnership
GB.description: A partnership with limited liability for its members.

GB.name: General Partnership
GB.description: A traditional partnership with shared liability.

GB.name: Sole Trader
GB.description: A self-employed individual running their own business.

GB.name: Community Interest Company
GB.description: A company designed for social enterprises.

GB.name: Charitable Incorporated Organisation
GB.description: A charity with a corporate structure.

GB.name: Cooperative
GB.description: A business owned and democratically controlled by its members.

SG.name: Private Limited Company
SG.description: The most common business structure in Singapore.

SG.name: Public Limited Company
SG.description: A company whose shares can be publicly traded.

SG.name: Sole Proprietorship
SG.description: A business owned and operated by a single individual.

SG.name: General Partnership
SG.description: A partnership with shared liability among partners.

SG.name: Limited Partnership
SG.description: A partnership with limited and general partners.

SG.name: Limited Liability Partnership
SG.description: A partnership with limited liability for its members.

SG.name: Variable Capital Company
SG.description: A corporate structure for investment funds.

AU.name: Proprietary Limited Company
AU.description: The most common company type in Australia with limited liability.

AU.name: Public Company
AU.description: A company that can offer shares to the public.

AU.name: Partnership
AU.description: A business structure involving two or more individuals.

AU.name: Trust
AU.description: A legal arrangement for holding and managing assets.

AU.name: Cooperative
AU.description: A business owned by its members.

AU.name: Not-for-Profit
AU.description: An organisation that does not operate for profit.

MY.name: Private Limited Company
MY.description: A privately held company with limited liability.

MY.name: Public Limited Company
MY.description: A company whose shares can be traded publicly.

MY.name: Sole Proprietorship
MY.description: A business owned and operated by a single person.

MY.name: Partnership
MY.description: A business formed by two or more partners.

MY.name: Limited Liability Partnership
MY.description: A partnership with limited liability for its members.
